articles.title: Weather Today [30 May, 2026]: Check IMD Rain Predictions, Heat Alert, Monsoon & Thunderstorms Across Delhi NCR, Mumbai, Chennai, Karnataka, Rajasthan, Jammu, Hyderabad & More — Full City-Wise Forecast - The Sunday Guardian
articles.full_text:
India Weather Update Today: India’s weather pattern is undergoing a dramatic shift after weeks of extreme heat. Several northern and central states that recently recorded temperatures above 45°C are now witnessing thunderstorms, gusty winds and scattered rainfall. The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has predicted a gradual easing of heatwave conditions across many regions, although parts of Rajasthan and western India remain under intense heat stress while, monsoon activity continues to advance across the Arabian Sea and Bay of Bengal.
Will it Rain or Snow in the Hills?
Higher reaches of Jammu and Kashmir, Himachal Pradesh and Uttarakhand may witness light rain, thunderstorms and isolated snowfall over very high-altitude regions.
- Srinagar experience Minimum at 11°C & Maximum at 17°C
- Tourist movement could face brief disruptions in hilly areas
Why Is The Heat Building Up So Fast Across India?
India’s rapid temperature rise is being driven by multiple climate and weather factors.
- Persistent high-pressure systems trapped hot air over northern India
- Several cities recorded temperatures above 46°C
- Climate scientists estimate global temperatures have already risen nearly 1.5°C since pre-industrial times
- Delayed monsoon progression has prolonged heat stress in several states
Recent studies suggest heatwaves of this intensity may become more frequent in coming decades.
Where are the Heat Wave Alerts?
IMD says heatwave intensity is expected to reduce gradually due to incoming thunderstorms and western disturbances.
Is Monsoon Arrived in India?
The southwest monsoon has advanced over parts of the Arabian Sea and Bay of Bengal.
- Conditions remain favorable for further advancement
- Kerala and coastal regions are witnessing increasing rainfall activity
- Moisture flow has strengthened across southern India
- Monsoon expansion is expected during the next few days
However, IMD recently warned that the overall 2026 monsoon may remain below average due to developing El Niño conditions.

FAQ’s
1. Are there any travel or safety warnings?
Yes. IMD has issued alerts for thunderstorms, strong winds, lightning and dust storms across several states. Travelers should monitor local advisories.
2. Will temperatures rise across the country?
Some regions may continue seeing temperatures above 40°C, but widespread heatwave intensity is expected to reduce.
3. Will there be any rain today?
Yes. Rain and thunderstorm activity is forecast across Delhi-NCR, Karnataka, Uttar Pradesh, Northeast India and parts of southern India.
4. Where will it snow today?
Only isolated high-altitude regions of Jammu and Kashmir and upper Himalayan zones may experience light snowfall.
5. Has the monsoon arrived in India?
The monsoon has advanced into parts of the Arabian Sea and Bay of Bengal and is expected to expand further over the coming days.
